_comment_purpose: This file declares MCP servers that EVERY teammate working in this
repository should have available. It's checked into git, so the team
1. ENV VAR EXPANSION — Values like ${GITHUB_TOKEN} are expanded by
Claude Code at startup. NEVER hardcode secrets in this file. The
convention: each developer sets the env var locally (in their
shell or via direnv); the placeholder shows what variable is
2. DESCRIPTIONS — Each server has a description. Claude Code uses
that to decide WHEN to invoke the server. A bad description ('A
useful tool') means Claude Code will call the wrong server. Be
3. PROJECT vs PERSONAL — Anything experimental or personal goes in
YOUR ~/.claude.json, NOT here. See personal-mcp-example.json for
(Some MCP server runtimes ignore _comment_* keys; if yours doesn't,

github._description: Read-only GitHub access for issues, PRs, and code search across the org.

postgres-readonly._description: Read-only access to the local development database. Use for ad-hoc queries while debugging — NEVER for application code (which goes through the repository pattern).

team-docs._description: The team's internal documentation site. Use for product specs, architecture decisions, runbooks. NOT for code — code lives in the repo.
